content.problemStatement:
Amr has given you an array AAA of size NNN and an integer XXX.

Let F(L,R)F(L, R)F(L,R) denote the number of pairs (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) such that:


L≤i<j≤RL \le i \lt j \le RL≤i<j≤R;
Ai+AjA_i + A_jAi​+Aj​ has **exactly** XXX digits.
Your task is to calculate the value ∑L=1N∑R=L+1NF(L,R)\displaystyle{\sum_{L=1}^N \sum_{R=L+1}^N F(L, R)}L=1∑N​R=L+1∑N​F(L,R).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the size of the array and the desired number of digits in the sum, respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, representing array elements.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the value ∑L=1N∑R=L+1NF(L,R)\displaystyle{\sum_{L=1}^N \sum_{R=L+1}^N F(L, R)}L=1∑N​R=L+1∑N​F(L,R).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2×1052 \times 10^52×105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are 555 valid pairs:

F(1,2)=1F(1, 2) = 1F(1,2)=1: The only pair is (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) resulting in A1+A2=10A_1+A_2=10A1​+A2​=10 with 222 digits.
F(1,3)=3F(1, 3) = 3F(1,3)=3: Pairs (1,2),(2,3),(1, 2), (2, 3),(1,2),(2,3), and (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) resulting in sum 10,19,10, 19,10,19, and 111111 respectively, each with 222 digits.
F(2,3)=1F(2, 3) = 1F(2,3)=1: The only pair is (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) resulting in A2+A3=19A_2+A_3=19A2​+A3​=19 with 222 digits.

Test case 222: There are 222 valid pairs:

F(1,2)=1F(1, 2) = 1F(1,2)=1: The only pair is (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) resulting in A1+A2=31A_1+A_2=31A1​+A2​=31 with 222 digits.
F(1,3)=1F(1, 3) = 1F(1,3)=1: The only pair is (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) resulting in A1+A2=31A_1+A_2=31A1​+A2​=31 with 222 digits.
F(2,3)=0F(2, 3) = 0F(2,3)=0

Test case 333: There are no valid pairs.
